globarAnalysisV2

ian enochs

2026-05-01

This document was created to analyze NCRMP BMU data

## Bars to remove due to ±3 SD outliers: 87

1.0.12 Graph deployment duration

1.0.13 Clean data

Remove data that is 1. Not successfully recovered 2. Not plob or mcav 3. Tag 1022 4. Data from Maug seep 5. Grazing data that is positive 6. Deployed for more than 6 years or less than 2 years 7. Depth is less than 10m

1.0.14 Remove vars with low replication

## ### Values masked (set to NA) where loc3 has < 3 non-NA measurements (per variable)
var n_locs_total n_locs_below_min
deltaMass_mgcm2y 64 6
deltaMass_kgm2y 64 6
deltaVolume_mm3cm2y 64 15
deltaDensity_mgcm3cm2y 64 21
macroboring_mm3cm2y 64 6
accretion_mm3cm2y 64 6
grazing_mm3cm2y 64 15

2 Data exploration

2.1 General

2.1.1 Map of BAR, parrot surveys, and environmental sites

2.1.2 Timeline of BAR data, parrot surveys, and environmental data

2.2 BAR data

2.2.1 Summary of locations and durations

Sites are divided at three levels across 2 basins. The highest level (loc1) includes 8 large regions including:
caribbean, flowerGardenBanks, florida, marianas, middleHawaiianIslands, pacificRemoteIslandArea, samoa, northwestHawaiianIslands

Within these larger regions are 20 sublevels (loc2) that are generally inclusive of an island including:
saintCroix, saintThomas, flowerGardenBanks, dryTortugas, keys, puertoRico, saintJohn, guam, hawaii, jarvis, maug, oahu, ofu, pagan, palmyra, pearlHermes, saipan, tau, tutuila, wake

Most islands or loc2 level includes multiple sites (loc3) within it, totaling 58 loc3 levels across both basins

Deployment dates ranged from 2014-03-25 to 2021-06-26 and recovery dates ranged from 2017-04-19 to 2024-08-24. The deployment duration ranged from 993 to 1950 days and was on 1381.5305623 days.

2.3 Parrot fish community

2.3.1 Summary

There are a total of 904 parrot surveys included in this study. Sites are divided at three levels across 2 basins. The highest level (loc1) includes 8 large regions including:
florida, flowerGardenBanks, caribbean, marianas, middleHawaiianIslands, northwestHawaiianIslands, pacificRemoteIslandArea, samoa

2.3.2 Unify parrot tables and calculate stats at loc3 level

2.5 Urban influence data

2.6 Calculates distance to nearest city directly at the loc3 level.

Note Loc2 is not done due to it being the average of multiple loc3 and therefore artificially not on a specific site
Closest city to each loc3 site
basin loc1 loc2 loc3 latitude longitude closest_city dist_city_km n_bar_rows n_unique_gps

2.7 Combine datasets

Combine bar stats and parrot community stats tables

3 Vertical dotplots for map by loc1 and loc2

#Drivers of carbonate persistence: Data were analyzed using linear models. Separate models were constructed for each basin, at two spatial scales, island-level and site-level, depending on the spatial resolution of the predictor data. Response parameters (Δmass, Δvolume, Δdensity, accretion, grazing, and macroboring) were grouped at the appropriate scale, resulting in an island or site-specific mean, used in the subsequent analysis. Candidate predictors were first analyzed for collinearity and models were constructed to maintain adequate residual degrees of freedom, and avoid strongly collinear predictors.

The effects of parrotfish grazing pressure and urban influence were analyzed at the site scale, with depth included as covariate in the Pacific. In the Atlantic, only parrotfish grazing pressure was used as a predictor due to strong collinearity among predictors, partly reflecting the lower number of Atlantic sites relative to the Pacific (8 vs. 37; Table SX; Figure SXX). Environmental predictors were evaluated at the island level, with chlorophyll and aragonite saturation state considered in the Atlantic, and depth added to the two parameters in the Pacific. Temperature (satellite-derived) was not evaluated due to collinearity with aragonite saturation state and mean site depth in both basins.

3.1 Check collinearity among parrotfish pressure, Urban Influence, and depth

Collinearity among loc3-level predictors by Basin
Basin Predictor comparison n Correlation p-value
Atlantic Parrotfish pressure vs. Depth 8 0.714 4.67e-02
Atlantic Parrotfish pressure vs. Urban Influence 8 0.972 5.15e-05
Atlantic Urban Influence vs. Depth 8 0.590 1.24e-01
Pacific Parrotfish pressure vs. Depth 37 0.166 3.25e-01
Pacific Parrotfish pressure vs. Urban Influence 37 0.079 6.43e-01
Pacific Urban Influence vs. Depth 37 -0.119 4.84e-01

4 Run linear models for Loc2 site-level analysis

Linear models were used in each basin to investigate the impact of aragonite saturation state on BAR responses. Models were run on loc2-averaged values, with depth and chlorophyll included as covariates. After depth and chlorophyll were accounted for, omega relationships were evaluated separately for each response variable and basin.

Note sites like St. Thomas and Wake with no density are not included in the analysis only of those parameters, as listed with NAs.

This is what ChatGPT told me about removing depth (don’t use): “Depth was not a predictor of interest, it did not improve interpretation, and including it costs degrees of freedom in a small loc2-level dataset.”
